content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a function that uses a generator expression to convert a given string into alternating uppercase and lowercase characters. The function should take a string as input and return a new string where the characters alternate between uppercase and lowercase, starting with uppercase.

For example, if the input is "hello world", the output should be "HeLlO WoRlD".

Your task is to implement the alternating_case function using a generator expression. The function should work for any input string, preserving spaces and special characters.
constraints: S may contain spaces, digits, punctuation and any printable ASCII characters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S (1 ≤ |S| ≤ 1000) which may include spaces and special characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing the transformed string where characters alternate between uppercase and lowercase, starting with uppercase.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each character is converted to upper case when its position is even (starting from 0) and to lower case when its position is odd.
Digits are unchanged; letters follow the alternating rule.
Non‑alphabetic characters stay the same after case conversion.

solution.approach: For each test case iterate over the characters of the input string with their index. If the index is even, output the character in uppercase, otherwise in lowercase. Non‑alphabetic characters remain unchanged because converting them to upper or lower case yields the same character. The transformation can be expressed concisely with a generator expression (or equivalent) and then joined into the result string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) – each character is visited once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the output string (the result is built character by character).
